resources.string:
Each SpoolTag format uses a different NFC chip. Buying the wrong sticker is the most common reason writes fail. Search the exact phrases below on Amazon, AliExpress, or NFC specialty shops.

Sticker size: 25 mm round is the usual spool label. NTAG stickers are ISO14443A (Type 2). MIFARE Classic tags are ISO14443A (Type A) but are not NTAG — do not mix them up.

Phone requirement: Your phone must support NFC. Samsung Galaxy S21 and newer (including S23) support MIFARE Classic. Some budget phones only read NTAG.

What to buy:
• "NTAG213 NFC sticker 25mm"
• "NTAG215 NFC tag blank round"

Minimum: NTAG213 (504 bytes user memory).
UID: 7-byte UID is fine.

Avoid: MIFARE Classic, NTAG216-only if you also use other NTAG213 workflows.

Where to buy:
• Amazon — search "NTAG213 sticker"
• AliExpress — "NTAG213 25mm NFC"
• GoToTags, NFC Tagify (US), Lab401 (EU)

What to buy:
• "NTAG213 NFC sticker" (most common blank)
• "MIFARE Ultralight NFC tag 25mm"

Official Anycubic ACE spools use MIFARE Ultralight; blank NTAG213 stickers work for custom writes in SpoolTag.
UID: 7-byte UID is fine.

Avoid: MIFARE Classic 1K cards.

Where to buy:
• Amazon — "NTAG213 NFC labels"
• AliExpress — "Ultralight NFC sticker"

What to buy:
• "NTAG215 NFC sticker"
• "NTAG216 NFC tag blank"

Required: NTAG215 minimum — NTAG213 is too small and SpoolTag will refuse the write.
Memory: NTAG215 ≈ 504 bytes NDEF; NTAG216 ≈ 888 bytes.

Works with: Bambu printers running OpenSpool firmware, Snapmaker U1, general inventory.

Where to buy:
• Amazon — "NTAG215 blank NFC"
• GoToTags — NTAG215 on-metal or standard stickers
• AliExpress — "NTAG216 25mm"

What to buy:
• "NTAG213 NFC sticker" — basic core data only
• "NTAG215 NFC tag" — recommended for full temp ranges
• "NTAG216 NFC tag" — maximum extended fields

Minimum: NTAG213 for core 112-byte map.
Best value: NTAG215 for extended temperature fields.

Where to buy:
• Amazon / AliExpress — same NTAG sticker listings as OpenSpool
• Any blank NTAG215/216 from NXP-compatible sellers

What to buy:
• "MIFARE Classic 1K S50 NFC card"
• "FM11RF08 blank tag" (common chip)
• "MIFARE 1K writable sticker" (if available in 25mm)

UID: 4-byte UID strongly recommended. Seven-byte UID tags may not work with Creality encryption.
Size: 1K (1024 bytes) — not Ultralight, not NTAG.

Note: Newer Creality factory tags use per-tag encryption. Blanks start unencrypted; first write locks sector keys.

Where to buy:
• Amazon — "MIFARE Classic 1K blank"
• AliExpress — "MIFARE 1K S50 writable"
• RFID Chentian, Sunorient (AliExpress NFC stores)

What to buy:
• "MIFARE Classic 1K blank white card"
• "MIFARE 1K NFC tag writable"
• Keyfob tags can work if thin enough for your spool hub

Data: Only 3 bytes in sector 1 — any blank 1K Classic tag is fine.
UID: 4-byte or 7-byte usually works for QIDI.

Avoid: NTAG213/215 stickers (wrong chip family).

Where to buy:
• Amazon — "MIFARE Classic 1K"
• AliExpress — "FM11RF08 writable"
Official tag: factory MIFARE Classic 1K (read-only in SpoolTag)

Reading: Use the RFID sticker already on official Bambu Lab spools. You cannot create new Bambu AMS tags from scratch — they are RSA-signed.

Clone blanks only (advanced):
• "MIFARE Classic 1K Gen2 magic tag"
• "FUID UID-changeable MIFARE 1K"
• "UID writable MIFARE Classic S50"

Must be Gen2/FUID magic — Gen1 UID-magic tags can be bricked by AMS firmware.
UID: 4-byte. Clone copies the source tag UID byte-for-byte.

Where to buy clone blanks:
• Amazon — "MIFARE UID changeable card"
• AliExpress — "FUID MIFARE 1K Gen2"
• RFID specialist shops (RFIDtag, TagNFC sellers)

NTAG (Type 2): Elegoo, Anycubic, OpenSpool, OpenTag3D
Look for: NTAG213, NTAG215, NTAG216

MIFARE Classic 1K: Creality, QIDI, Bambu
Look for: MIFARE Classic 1K, S50, FM11RF08

Wrong chip = scan works on nothing or write fails silently.
